common.tagline: One Chrome extension. Eleven social networks. Zero hassle.
common.footerContact: Tutti is a Chrome extension by komm64. Contact: contact@komm64.com

index.title: Tutti — Cross-post extension
index.headerTagline: One Chrome extension. Eleven social networks. Zero hassle.
index.overviewBody: Tutti broadcasts the same post to 11 social networks (X, Bluesky, Threads, Tumblr, Mastodon, Misskey, Pixiv, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram, DeviantArt) in one click. It has no backend; post content never passes through any third-party server.
index.privacySummaryBody: Tutti has no backend server. Post content (text, images, video) goes directly from your browser to each social network you select — never via any developer-controlled server.

privacy.title: Tutti — Privacy Policy
privacy.headerTagline: No third-party data collection. All post content goes directly from your browser to each SNS.
privacy.dataIntro: Tutti has no backend server. Post content (text, images, video) is never transmitted to any third-party server. It travels directly from your browser to each social network you select, exactly as if you had typed and submitted it there yourself.
privacy.dataLi1: Post text, images, and video are passed only to the SNS pages you choose (X, Bluesky, Threads, Mastodon, Misskey, Tumblr, Pixiv, DeviantArt, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube) within your browser.
privacy.dataLi2: When the optional official-API path is enabled for Bluesky / Mastodon / Misskey, posts are sent directly from your browser to that SNS's own API server using credentials you supplied in Settings. No Tutti / developer server is involved.
privacy.dataLi3: No analytics, tracking, or advertising data is collected.
privacy.dataLi4: Tutti does not read or store your authentication tokens, cookies, or session data from the SNS sites.
privacy.h3Report: Exception — manual error reports (opt-in per click)
privacy.reportIntro: When (and only when) you press the “Report” button shown in an error dialog or the diagnose panel, the following is sent through a Cloudflare Workers relay to a private GitHub issue tracker (komm64/tutti-issues, viewable only by the Tutti developer):
privacy.reportLi1: The error message, extension version, browser User-Agent string.
privacy.reportLi2: The last 30 entries of the extension's internal log (the log level you configured; INFO by default). Post content, images, video, SNS login data, and API credentials are never included.
privacy.reportLi3: A redacted DOM snapshot of the SNS tab where the error occurred, used to repair broken selectors. Text content, attribute values, URL paths/queries/fragments, href/src attributes, and <meta>/<link> tags are stripped. The payload is capped at 8 KB. See dom-snapshot.ts for the implementation.
privacy.reportOutro: Nothing is sent until you press the report button. The destination is private, viewable only by the Tutti developer.
privacy.localIntro: The following is stored only inside your browser via Chrome's storage API. Nothing is transmitted off-device by Tutti.
privacy.localSettings: Settings (chrome.storage.sync): Mastodon / Misskey instance URLs, autoPost toggle, display mode, selector override URL, etc. May be replicated to your other devices via Chrome Sync if you are signed into Chrome.
privacy.localDrafts: Drafts (chrome.storage.session): In-progress post text and attached media (images / video). Auto-cleared when the browser exits.
privacy.localSelected: Selected SNS (chrome.storage.local): The SNS you ticked as post targets. Persists across sessions for convenience; device-local only.
privacy.localUsers: Last seen usernames (chrome.storage.local): The currently-logged-in username on each SNS, displayed in the popup so you can confirm which account will receive the post. Device-local only.
privacy.localHistory: Post history (chrome.storage.local): The last 20 posts: first 80 characters of text, target platforms, success/failure per platform, and timestamp. Device-local only.
privacy.localCreds: API credentials (chrome.storage.local): Only if you explicitly enabled the official-API path for Bluesky / Mastodon / Misskey: your Bluesky app password or Mastodon / Misskey access token. Device-local only; never replicated via Chrome Sync; never transmitted to any developer server. Removable from the Settings wizard.
privacy.localSelectors: Selector override cache (chrome.storage.local): Selector hot-fix data fetched from the URL you configured in Settings (default: https://tutti.komm64.com/selectors.json).
privacy.localVideoCap: Video upload limit cache (chrome.storage.local): Result of API probes for per-account video upload caps (e.g., Bluesky). Numeric values only; no credentials.
privacy.localWipe: All of this can be wiped at any time by uninstalling the extension or clearing Chrome's extension storage.
privacy.permsStorage: Store the local data above (settings, drafts, history, selected platforms, etc.) inside your browser.
privacy.permsOffscreen: Run ffmpeg.wasm in an offscreen document to compress / re-encode video files when the source exceeds the destination SNS's size cap. Video data is processed locally only; not transmitted anywhere by Tutti.
privacy.permsSidePanel: Optional UI display mode: show Tutti's compose form in Chrome's side panel instead of the action popup, so it stays open while you switch between SNS tabs. You opt in via the Options page; default is the standard popup.
privacy.permsHost: Inject content scripts into each SNS site (x.com / twitter.com / bsky.app / bsky.social / threads.net / threads.com / mastodon.social / misskey.io / tumblr.com / pixiv.net / tiktok.com / youtube.com / instagram.com / deviantart.com) to fill the compose form with your post content and submit it, on your behalf. No data is read from these sites and sent elsewhere; the content script only writes your own input.
privacy.permsOptHost: Requested at runtime only if you configure a custom Mastodon or Misskey instance URL (other than the defaults). Chrome shows the standard permission prompt at that moment.
privacy.relationshipBody: Tutti automates the web UI of (or, optionally, posts via the official API of) the 11 supported networks. This is unofficial functionality; Tutti is not affiliated with X, Bluesky, Meta (Threads / Instagram), Mastodon gGmbH, Misskey, Tumblr, Pixiv, DeviantArt, TikTok, or YouTube. You remain bound by each service's own terms of use.
privacy.changesBody: Updates to this policy are communicated by updating this page and the canonical source file (PRIVACY.md).

support.title: Tutti — Support
support.failedBody: Use the “Diagnose” → “Report” button inside the popup. This is the canonical channel. The report is redacted (handles, URLs, emails) and forwarded to our private issue tracker, where a selector fix PR is opened automatically and pushed as a hot-fix to all users within days.
support.calloutTitle: Why use the in-product Report button?
support.calloutBody: The popup's Report flow automatically attaches the final compose state, a redacted DOM snapshot, and selector audit results — exactly what's needed to fix selector breakage fast. PII is stripped through multiple layers before sending.
support.generalBody: Email us at contact@komm64.com. This is a private channel, safe to share screenshots / personal handles with.
support.installBody: Tutti reuses your existing browser logins for each social network. There's no separate Tutti account or login step. Once you're logged in to a social network in the same Chrome profile, Tutti can post from there.
support.a1: This usually means a social network changed its DOM. Use Diagnose → Report in the popup. A selector hot-fix usually ships within days via selectors.json auto-update (no extension update required).
support.q2: Q. Tutti doesn't remember my login.
support.a2: Tutti relies on the network's normal browser login. Log in to each network in Chrome first, then Tutti can post on your behalf. Incognito mode doesn't work because the network sessions aren't there.
support.q3: Q. Character limits differ per network — is that a problem?
support.a3: No. Long posts that exceed a network's character limit are automatically split into a continuation thread (on X, a proper reply chain; on other networks, sequential posts). Posts within the limit go through as-is.
support.q4: Q. I'm told my image / video is too large.
support.a4: Images are auto-resized via Canvas to fit each network's size limit. Videos are auto-compressed with ffmpeg.wasm when above a network's cap. If even compression can't fit, you'll see an error — please verify the file uploads via the network's native app first.
support.q5: Q. Does Tutti see my post content?
support.a5: No. Post content never passes through a third-party server. Tutti injects directly into each network's web compose page. See our Privacy Policy for details. The only exception is when you press the in-product Report button, which sends redacted error information to our private issue tracker.
support.a6: Yes! The biggest ongoing cost is keeping up with each social network's UI changes. Any support is appreciated:
